what: Package to Facilitate a Simulation Study on Causal Inference Methods in RCTs
title: Package to Facilitate a Simulation Study on Causal Inference Methods in RCTs
text: repository contains code simulation study investigating causial inference (CI) methods used account intercurrent events (ICE) randomized controlled trials (RCT).

title: Package to Facilitate a Simulation Study on Causal Inference Methods in RCTs
text: install git clone repository open rstudio project install dependencies renv::restore()

title: Package to Facilitate a Simulation Study on Causal Inference Methods in RCTs
text: ’re using Rstudio: start R root folder project working directory. renv loaded automatically see message like Project '...' loaded. [renv 1.1.5] able use renv::restore(). renv automatically loaded use source("renv/activate.R") load .

title: Package to Facilitate a Simulation Study on Causal Inference Methods in RCTs
text: Reusable functions, methods, etc. available r-package (built repository), can tested, built, installed, distributed. Bespoke code simulation study included directory scripts.

title: Package to Facilitate a Simulation Study on Causal Inference Methods in RCTs
text: contribute code: switch main branch git checkout main make sure latest version main branch git pull main create new branch git checkout -b <new_branch_name> (replace  name branch) push code github git push -u origin <new_branch_name> first time just git push afterwards merge code main branch, open pull request github. Navigate branch use “Compare & pull request” button.

title: Package to Facilitate a Simulation Study on Causal Inference Methods in RCTs
text: minimal working example contained following files: R/generate_minimal_example.R: definition parameter values data generation R/analyse_mimial_example.R: definition two example analysis function work generated datasets scripts/minimal_example.R: calling functions defined theSimDesign package helper functions SimNPH package

title: Analyse Dataset with the Inverse probability weighting — analyse_ipw
text: analyse function returns list elements p p value score test (two.sided) Wald test (one.sided) alternative alternative used coef coefficient trt hr hazard ratio trt hr_lower lower 95% confidence intervall boundary hazard ratio trt hr_upperlower 95% confidence intervall boundary hazard ratio trt CI_level CI level used N_pat number patients N_evt number events

title: Analyse Dataset with the Inverse probability weighting — analyse_ipw
text: alternative can "two.sided" two sided test equality summary statistic "one.sided" one sided test testing H0: treatment equal shorter survival control vs. H1 treatment longer survival control.

text: ci_level confidence level CIs (defaults 0.95)

title: Analyse dataset from minimal example scenario with linear regression — analyse_minimal_example_lm
text: analyse function returns list elements p p-value F-test coef point estimate ci_lower lower CI limit ci_upper upper CI limit

title: Analyse dataset from minimal example scenario with linear regression — analyse_minimal_example_lm
code: Design <- assumptions_minimal_example() |>   true_summary_statistics_minimal_example()  condition <- Design[1, ]  dat <- generate_minimal_example(condition)  my_analyse_lm <- analyse_minimal_example_lm(ci_level=0.9) my_analyse_lm(condition, dat) #> $p #> [1] 0.0001640878 #>  #> $coef #>    group  #> 0.734723  #>  #> $ci_lower #> [1] 0.4234935 #>  #> $ci_upper #> [1] 1.045952 #>

code: params_scenarios_grid(x=1:3, y=11:12, z=21) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>       x     y     z #>   <int> <int> <dbl> #> 1     1    11    21 #> 2     2    11    21 #> 3     3    11    21 #> 4     1    12    21 params_scenarios_grid(x=1:2, y=list(c(1), c(1, 2))) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>       x y         #>   <int> <list>    #> 1     1 <dbl [1]> #> 2     2 <dbl [1]> #> 3     1 <dbl [2]>
